methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 59.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Argotec, a growth-stage space and aerospace company founded by David Avino, is targeting €75M in this funding round. The valuation universe comprises 13 identifiable peers—5 public companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private entities including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace—though none form a sufficiently tight peer cohort for conventional median-based valuation.

Peer-median triangulation is inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow space and aerospace subsegment. Of 23 same-sector peers identified, none satisfied the minimum threshold of 3+ datapoints in Argotec's valuation tier and development stage. Public-comp anchoring, while theoretically available given a 59.8x P/S median across the six public tickers, cannot be executed because Argotec's revenue is not disclosed—standard practice for private companies. Both primary triangulation methods therefore yield no quantitative output.

The absence of applicable valuation methods underscores material information gaps rather than analytical failure. A defensible valuation range would require either: disclosure of Argotec's revenue and profitability metrics to enable public-comp P/S or EV/EBITDA anchoring; identification of 3+ closer-stage private peers with disclosed terms; or forward financial projections to support DCF modeling. Until such data surfaces, no numerical valuation recommendation can be responsibly derived from the current dataset.
